title: virrun configuration
description: The repo-root virrun.config.json: a checked-in, reviewable allowlist of which commands run through the sandbox and which backend they use (adoption level 3). Every field is optional and falls back to a safe default, so a minimal { "route": ["vitest run"] } is valid; an absent file means nothing routes and virrun is a no-op.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this JSON schema, for editor autocompletion and validation.

route.description: Commands that route through the sandbox, matched by leading tokens — `vitest run` matches `vitest run --coverage` but not bare `vitest`. Everything not listed stays native (per-command granularity). Defaults to none.

backend.description: The backend a routed command runs through. Defaults to `auto` (resolves to native today). Mirror of the BackendType enum in src/models/virrun/BackendType.ts.
enumDescriptions: Pick the best backend the host supports — native today, until an isolating backend beats the gates.
Run the command directly on the host with no virtualization.
Run inside a bubblewrap RAM-overlay (Linux, or Windows via WSL2): reads fall through to source, writes vanish in tmpfs. Ephemeral — only safe for read-only verification commands.
Run recognised pure-JS node invocations in-process; falls back to native for everything else. Opt-in, no isolation.

fallback.description: The backend to defer to when `backend` can't run on this host (e.g. `os` off Linux/WSL). Never errors the build — worst case is no speedup. Defaults to `native`.
